chunks.text: Susan L. Graham is the Pehong Chen Distinguished Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science Emerita at UC Berkeley. She received an A.B. in mathematics from Harvard University and M.S. and Ph.D. degrees in Computer Science from Stanford University. Her research spans programming language implementation, software tools, software development environments, and high-performance computing. As a participant in the Berkeley Unix project, she and her students built the Berkeley Pascal system and the widely used program profiling tool gprof.
chunks.question: What are Susan Graham's educational background and key research contributions?
chunks.answer: Harvard math AB, Stanford CS PhD; built Berkeley Pascal and gprof.

chunks.text: She is a Member of the National Academy of Engineering, a Fellow of ACM, AAAS, American Academy of Arts and Sciences, and IEEE, and an Eminent Member of Eta Kappa Nu. She was the founding editor-in-chief of ACM Transactions on Programming Languages and Systems. Her honors include ACM SIGPLAN Career Programming Language Achievement Award (2000), ACM Distinguished Service Award (2006), Harvard Medal (2008), IEEE von Neumann Medal (2009), Berkeley Citation (2009), ACM/IEEE Ken Kennedy Award (2011), and CRA Distinguished Service Award (2012).
chunks.question: What professional memberships and major awards has Susan Graham received?
chunks.answer: NAE member, ACM/IEEE fellow; von Neumann Medal, Kennedy Award.

chunks.text: She has served on numerous advisory committees including U.S. President's Information Technology Advisory Committee (PITAC), Harvard Board of Overseers, Curtis Institute of Music Board of Overseers, Harvard Corporation, and President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (PCAST). She served as Chief Computer Scientist for NPACI from 1997 to 2005, and as Vice-Chair then Chair of the Computing Community Consortium. She is co-Chair of the Board of Trustees of Cal Performances.
chunks.question: What advisory roles and leadership positions has Susan Graham held?
chunks.answer: PITAC, PCAST member; NPACI Chief Scientist; Cal Performances co-Chair.

chunks.text: Her research areas include Graphics (GR), Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), Programming Systems (PS), Scientific Computing (SCI), and software development environments. Selected publications include work on Titanium language, supercomputing future, gprof profiler, BPF+ packet filters, compiler transformations, and efficient software-based fault isolation spanning 1976-2006.
chunks.question: What are Susan Graham's research areas and publication highlights?
chunks.answer: Programming systems, HCI; Titanium, gprof, compiler transformations publications.
